articles.title: Vercel Acquires NuxtLabs
articles.description: Vercel has acquired the company that caretakes the Nuxt project and employs some of its core team – a move Vue creator Evan You is quite optimistic about. Vercel now manages, or at least supports, several key projects like Next.js, Turborepo, Svelte, and shadcn/ui. Nuxt itself remains open source and has a promising future. Vercel’s Guillermo Rauch shares a little more about the move here.
articles.author: NuxtLabs / Vercel

articles.title: A Detailed Summary of the Latest TC39 Plenary
articles.description: A thorough roundup of May’s major ECMAScript committee meeting with far more detail about each proposal’s development and the decisions made than we usually get to hear about. Topics include Array.fromAsync, explicit resource management, the Temporal API, and some brainstorming around AsyncContext.
articles.author: Igalia Compilers Team

articles.title: What’s the Difference Between Ordinary Functions and Arrow Functions?
articles.description: This sounds like basic stuff, but James always does a good job of digging in and explaining things in a way that gives you a more nuanced way to think about a concept, even if it’s just “Which function declaration syntax should I use?”
articles.author: James Sinclair

articles.title: JavaScript Scope Hoisting is Broken
articles.description: The creator of Parcel argues that scope hoisting (when bundlers inline modules into a shared scope) conflicts with modern JS patterns like code splitting and dynamic imports, causing subtle bugs and offering little benefit, so he’s considering removing it in Parcel v3.
articles.author: Devon Govett

articles.title: Codepoint–Safe Truncation: Fixing Emoji Slicing
articles.description: An app’s CSV importer kept breaking on emoji-filled rows, triggering errors. James demonstrates how swapping slice for a code-point–aware spread fixes it.
articles.author: James Mulholland

articles.title: Driver.js: Tours, Highlights, Contextual Help, and More
articles.description: A vanilla JS library for making on-page tours and contextual help systems. It’s been around for several years, but is still maintained, and there are lots of examples to check out – it’s really smooth.
articles.author: Kamran Ahmed

articles.title: jsonrepair: Repair Invalid JSON Documents
articles.description: This has lots of possible use cases, including dealing with weird JSON coming back from LLMs or non-compliant JSON spat out by poorly built software. You can use it from Node, as a CLI tool, or try a basic version online.
articles.author: Jos de Jong

articles.title: line-numbers: A Web Component to Add Line Numbers Next to Various HTML Elements
articles.description: Useful for custom apps that show source code or other snippets that require line numbering. See examples here, which demonstrate the flexibility customization options for the line numbering.
articles.author: Zach Leatherman

articles.title: cRonstrue 3.0: Convert Cron Expressions into Natural Language
articles.description: Not just English either - it supports about thirty locales. There’s also an online demo.
articles.author: Brady Holt

articles.description: TypeScript 5.9 Beta has been released with support for deferred module evaluation by using import defer.

The Nginx HTTP server has a module (njs) for extending its functionality using JavaScript, but it only supported ES5. Now, njs uses QuickJS for a more modern, powerful experience with full ES2023 support.
Got some time to kill? There's one week left to submit an entry to the JS1024 annual JavaScript golfing competition. Create a cool JS or GLSL program in under 1024 bytes.
Oliver Stenbom reflects on the prevalence of Rust in modern JavaScript ecosystem tooling.
Node.js v24.4.0 (Current) – Note that new versions of all maintained Node.js releases are due next week due to some security vulnerabilities.
Oxlint 1.6 – Rust-powered linter for JS and TS.
VS Code v1.102, Ember 6.5, Angular 20.1

articles.title: CSS Conditionals with the New if() Function
articles.description: The if function opens up a slew of architectural opportunities for CSS. Here’s a look at how we can now use such inline conditionals along with media, supports, and style queries – in Chrome 137+ at least, though support should grow given the CSS WG's consensus on the feature. Demos and code aplenty.
articles.author: Una Kravets

articles.title: 'We Studied 100 Dev Tool Landing Pages, Here’s What Really Works in 2025'
articles.description: You're bound to find a takeaway or two from here that will prove useful – a good roundup of modern day landing pages, even.
articles.author: Anton Lovchikov

articles.title: CSS Intelligence: Speculating on the Future of a Smarter Language
articles.description: Thanks to a growing number of ‘logical powers’, Gabriel explores how CSS is evolving to be something of a smarter language. His post looks at where things are heading, asks if things are becoming too complex, and prods at how developers are reacting to this shift.
articles.author: Gabriel Shoyombo

articles.title: What is popover=hint?
articles.description: The hint popover type arrived earlier this year, and here Una breaks it down, complete with animated visuals showing the stacking ability in action, and code examples. Una notes features such as this “make building layered UI elements that are currently really hard to implement much easier”.
articles.author: Una Kravets

articles.title: Better CSS Shapes Using shape(): close and move
articles.description: Temani wraps up their great series on getting the most out of the shape() function, this time by focusing in on two commands that you’ll likely rarely use, but are “incredibly useful when you need them”.
articles.author: Temani Afif

articles.title: How to Create Kinetic Image Animations with React Three Fiber
articles.description: A fantastic visual effect for the Web (which can try live here) that’s explained in technical detail. It’s amazing with React Three Fiber pull off and might inspire you to have a play for yourself.
articles.author: Dominik Fojcik

articles.title: line-numbers: A Web Component to Add Line Numbers Next to Various HTML Elements
articles.description: Useful for custom apps that show source code or other snippets that require line numbering. See examples here, which demonstrate the flexibility customization options for the line numbering.
articles.author: Zach Leatherman

articles.title: Monorail: Turn CSS Keyframe Animations into an Interactive Graph
articles.description: A potentially useful visualization for something you might not inherently get a feel for otherwise. Play with the demo on the main page to get the idea.
articles.author: Stanko

articles.title: snapDOM 1.8: Captures DOM Nodes as Images
articles.description: A rapidly maturing, fast and accurate DOM-to-image capture mechanism to capture any HTML element as a scalable SVG image, preserving styles, fonts, background images, etc. The homepage is packed with examples.
articles.author: ZumerLab

articles.title: Vecto3d: A Tools to Convert SVGs to 3D Models
articles.description: There are some example SVG logos you can try it out with (such as the Octocat here). Includes a number of customization options to play with the look (texture, bevel, etc), and you can export in different formats. Repo here.
articles.author: Lakshay Bhushan

articles.title: CSS to Tailwind Converter: Convert CSS Gradients to Tailwind Classes
articles.description: Paste your CSS code and the tool will generate the Tailwind-compatible version. Supports linear, radial, and conic gradients and there’s a gradient library of ready-to-use snippets.
articles.author: Jack Higar

articles.title: URL to Any: An All-in-One Conversion Tool
articles.description: This tool lets you convert or extract the content of a page by entering the URL. Will convert to Markdown, HTML, PDF, image, JSON, XML, or plain text.
articles.author: URL to Any
